storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
An ocean spirit who once faced demise in solitude has found a miraculous second life on land.

Whether naturally optimistic or choosing to forget to escape suffering, her guileless innocence exudes an irresistible charm that draws people to her side—perhaps this is a subtle ploy of hers to expand her "circle of friends."

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
During the investigation along the southern coast of Cornwall, I discovered a derelict village unmarked on any known map. Upon investigation, we confirmed that it had been "erased" by an outbreak of dissolution.

In the village, we collected a large number of anomalous marine specimens, including carnivorous giant clams, heat-resistant sea angels, and most baffling of all, "amphibious jellyfish" found everywhere.

In a dilapidated house that had become a jellyfish nest, we recovered a fluid Silver Core weighing approximately 11.85 drams. While extracting the core, our team was attacked by mutated jellyfish; fortunately, their glands had decayed, posing no neurotoxic threat.

—<Italic:Record of the Third Biology Department Field Survey, A.F. 308>

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Don't expect much from the intellect of jellyfish; Aurita cannot comprehend what she has experienced. All she can affirm is that she once lived in that deserted village. Whenever the past is brought up, she deflects the conversation by feigning understanding, so Aurita contributes little to the study of D-Phenomenon or cataclysmic anomalies.

However, as a rare harmless Awakener, Aurita is deeply cherished by the faculty and students of the Awakener Department. Even Professor Czort, the lead researcher in Dissolution biology, takes particular care of her.

By the way, she gained the ability to split and proliferate after merging with the jellyfish's collective consciousness. Even if what proliferates are just ordinary jellyfish, Aurita excitedly holds up her "companions" to boast door-to-door—though, unable to distinguish between individuals, she remains blissfully unaware that the "new friend" she presents each time is the exact same jellyfish.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
March
Welcome to the Atlantica Touring Aquarium! I'm Aurita, your guide for today—please watch your step as you board the jellyfish caravan!

Displayed here in the tanks are rare jellyfish from tropical waters. Ta-da! Please take a look—

April
Wh-what happened... Where did everyone go... Curator! Curator! The jellyfish—Aurita's jellyfish are all gone!

The aquarium never had jellyfish exhibits? But I clearly remember...

May
Since there are too few exhibits and no guests, we can't keep the business going... <Italic:sob>... Goodbye, everyone...

If—if we ever go on tour again someday, please come find Aurita!

June
Aurita is so tired... Am I sick...?

Oh...! The jellyfish! Sorry, Aurita can't scatter diatoms for you all right now.

Once Aurita feels better, let's all swim together again...

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
A fishing village with crystal-clear waters, a shoreline adorned like the Milky Way on a midsummer night, and the lighthearted laughter echoing from a touring caravan—these memories of a world that no longer exists flash back from time to time, only to be promptly cast aside.

As long as companions keep appearing by her side, new memories can always be made. Besides, the jellyfish are always there—at least, that is what Aurita believes.

After all, to drift and propagate, and face down formidable threats through sheer numbers, is both the nature and the destiny of the jellyfish.
